title: Public WiFi Safety
drDigitalIntro:
Public WiFi is convenient but less secure than home.
• Others on the network could see your data.
• Avoid banking or email on it; stick to https sites.
• A VPN adds protection. Reading the news is fine — just skip anything private.

playgroundTask.goal: You identified risks on public WiFi

steps.say: Inspect 'Free WiFi Login' — this link is from a suspicious sign-in portal.
The URL is unsecured. Mark it Dangerous.
Now go to Privacy and turn off Location when on public networks.

drDigitalSuccess: You identified public WiFi risks! Be cautious about what you access on public networks.
drDigitalHint: Inspect links for unsecured URLs and limit what personal info your device shares on public networks.
